title: 9 Depositing Cheques in Branch Capture
body_text:
9	Depositing Cheques in Branch Capture
9.1	Overview of Depositing Cheques in Branch Capture
Branch Capture deposit processing allows you to scan images of Canadian and US dollar cheques, drafts, money orders, and Receiver General Warrants and transmit them to Central 1. Physical items arenГўВҖВҷt sent to Central 1. You can view deposit item images in Branch Capture for 180 days after processing and in the Online Return System (ORS) for seven years.
There are four Branch Capture user types: Capture Users, Reviewers, Supervisors, and Administrators. Based on how you assign user types, a userГўВҖВҷs tasks, experiences, and responsibilities vary. See Section 8.2, Branch Capture Workflows and User Types, for more information. The procedures in this chapter primarily apply Capture Users and/or Capture Users who are also assigned other Branch Capture user types (e.g., Reviewer and/or Supervisor User). Where applicable, differences in user experiences based on user type combinations are noted.
Branch Capture deposits must be transmitted to Central 1 by 7:00 pm local time, or they are processed the following business day. Scan your deposits throughout the day and transmit your deposits to Central 1 at least three times a day (e.g., mid-morning, mid-afternoon, and end of day). DonГўВҖВҷt transmit all your deposits to Central 1 at the end of day.
Note:	When moving between pages in Branch Capture, use the application navigation features, donГўВҖВҷt use your browser navigation.
Training videos are available on the Client Centre.
Related Information
Training Videos: Branch Capture
9.2	Preparing Branch Capture Deposits
Prepare Canadian and US dollar items for Branch Capture deposit processing as follows:
Verify that each item is acceptable for deposit. See Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, for more information.
Note:	CPA Rule A1, General Rules Pertaining to Items Acceptable for Clearing, for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement, prohibits the exchange of paper items that exceed $25 million. This limit canГўВҖВҷt be avoided by splitting the payment into two or more items on the same day.
Verify the itemГўВҖВҷs elements (e.g., signature, date) and apply regular verification procedures. See Section 5.4, Verifying Information on Cheques.
Ensure that the deposit items arenГўВҖВҷt photocopies. Photocopies made by the branch or a depositing customer arenГўВҖВҷt allowed under Payments Canada Rules.
DonГўВҖВҷt pre-encode cheques.
Separate items into the following payment types:
ГўВҲВ’
Canadian dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders
ГўВҲВ’
US dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders
ГўВҖВ“ Includes Canadian dollar items drawn on a Canadian financial institution that has ГўВҖВңUS FundsГўВҖВқ or similar wording hand-written on the cheque to designate the items as US dollar.
Note:	The time required to clear a cheque through the US clearing system varies.
ГўВҲВ’
Canada Savings or Canada Premium Bonds
ГўВҖВ“ Must have a MICR.
Important
: 	See the Redeeming or Transferring Bonds Part for information on handling bonds for redemption.
ГўВҲВ’
Ontario Savings Bonds
ГўВҖВ“ Must have a MICR. These items are processed as Canadian dollar items and must not be mixed with Canada Savings, Canada Premium Bonds, or Canadian dollar items. Process as a separate deposit.
Further separate items that should be returned to a different financial institution if dishonoured. Create separate deposits for these deposit item. Users manually enter the return information (i.e., branch and institution number) for these deposit items in Branch Capture. See Section 9.11, Entering Return Information in Branch Capture, for more information.
ГўВҲВ’
ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs deposit items
ГўВҖВ“ See ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs Deposits (BC) Part for additional instructions on preparing ICBC agentГўВҖВҷs account items for deposit.
ГўВҲВ’
Receiver General Concentrator Account deposit items
ГўВҖВ“ See the Branch Capture ГўВҖВ“ Processing Receiver General Deposits for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Concentrator Account, Form 5976, for additional instructions on preparing Receiver General Concentrator holding account items for deposit.
ГўВҲВ’
Provincial Concentrator Account deposit items
ГўВҖВ“ See the Provincial Concentrator Account Deposits (BC) Part for additional instructions on preparing Provincial Concentrator holding account items for deposit.
ГўВҲВ’
Inter-institution deposit items
(e.g., non-member ATM deposits and ICU deposits processed in Branch Capture) ГўВҖВ“ When processing non-member ATM deposits, ensure that you comply with the appropriate networkГўВҖВҷs rules.
Repair damaged items with transparent tape and remove staples, paperclips, and perforated edges to avoid a scanner jam.
Write ГўВҖВңtaken as $XX.xxГўВҖВқ on the top-right corner of the item if the amount is unclear or is written in gel ink. DonГўВҖВҷt modify the amount, the date, or Payee field.
Keep deposits to 100 items or less. This makes it easier to balance deposits and find errors.
Optionally, stamp the back of each item with your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs identification stamp. This step isnГўВҖВҷt required because Central 1 automatically provides a virtual identification stamp on every deposit item.
Note: 	Some deposit items require a special stamp to be placed on the back of each item (e.g., Non-member ATM deposits).
If you use a manual stamp, ensure that
ГўВҲВ’
itГўВҖВҷs positioned so it doesnГўВҖВҷt obscure the virtual endorsement, which is placed in the bottom left on the reverse of the item, and
ГўВҲВ’
it complies with CPA Rule A3, Endorsement and Identification of Paper Items. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: ATM Stamp Placement
Note: 	Customers who regularly deposit a lot of cheques in a single deposit can stamp the cheques themselves.
Ensure that items are face forward, right side up, and that MICR characters are printed along the bottom of the item.
Group the items by size to help minimize scanner jams.
Create a tape listing of all items in the deposit.
Note:	If there are several tape listings in a deposit (e.g., several tellersГўВҖВҷ work is consolidated into a single deposit), create a recap tape listing to provide the grand total for the deposit. Ensure that the recap tape listing and its corresponding tape listings are stacked together with the recap tape listing on top. DonГўВҖВҷt
scan the tape listings. The tape listing and the corresponding deposit items should be kept together in secure storage in date order until they are destroyed.
Scan items in the same order as the tape listing.
Related Information
Branch Capture ГўВҖВ“ Processing Receiver General Deposits for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Concentrator Account,
Form 5976
9.3	Logging in to Branch Capture
To log in to Branch Capture:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 2.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 2: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Figure 3: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
Branch Capture
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Accessing Branch Capture
Important
:	Always log off the Client Centre and close the browser when you step away from your desk or when youГўВҖВҷre finished using the system.
9.4	Resolving Branch Capture Login Issues
If the message ГўВҖВңUser ID of Remote Request was InvalidГўВҖВқ displays when a Branch Capture User first logs in to the application, verify the following:
The userГўВҖВҷs profile is granted access to Branch Capture and the parameters are entered correctly in CPS Admin or User Management. See the CPS Admin Application Part or User Management Part for procedures.
Edits to the userГўВҖВҷs profile arenГўВҖВҷt pending the approval of a second User Management Security Officer. Go to the Dual Custody tab on CPS Admin or the Manage Requests option in User Management to verify. See the CPS Admin Application Part or User Management Part for procedures.
20 minutes have passed after the Branch Capture UserГўВҖВҷs profile is created or edited in CPS Admin or User Management. The profile changes need time to sync to the Client Centre.
9.5	Creating a Branch Capture Deposit
Before creating a Branch Capture deposit, ensure items are prepared and eligible for deposit. See Section 9.2, Preparing Branch Capture Deposits, for more information.
Note:	Include a maximum of 100 cheques per deposit.
Capture Users create a Branch Capture deposit as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Select
Create New Deposit
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select ГўВҖВңCreate New DepositГўВҖВқ
Complete the New Deposit page as follows. See Figure 6.
ГўВ‘
Select the deposit location. If you only have access to one location, the location will default.
Note:	The location that you select is the default location for the next deposit(s) you create during the current Branch Capture session. Select a different location if required.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the applicable deposit type for the items from the Account Number drop-down menu.
ГўВҲВ’
CAD-DB for Canadian dollar cheques and Ontario Savings Bonds
ГўВҲВ’
USD-DB for US dollar and US dollar cheques drawn on a Canadian financial institution
ГўВҲВ’
CSB-DB for Certificate Style (MICR encoded) Canada Savings and Canada Premium Bonds (Central 1 clients only)
ГўВ‘Вў
Complete Deposit Reference fields 1-3 (optional). Use the fields to enter information pertinent to the deposit (e.g., the deposit type, such as ГўВҖВңReceiver General DepositГўВҖВқ, or a teller number). Information that you enter in the Deposit Reference fields appears on the virtual deposit slip and the Branch Capture Deposit Item Details report. See Section 13.2, Generating a Branch Capture Deposit Item Details Report.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Enter the total of all deposit items in the Declared Amount field.
Place the deposit items in the scanner hopper, then select
Start Capture
.
Figure 6. Completing the New Deposit Page
As items are scanned, a Deposit Item List page displays and items begin streaming into the deposit. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Viewing the Deposit Item List
You must review the deposit to ensure that itГўВҖВҷs error-free and balanced. Additionally, you must manually enter return information if dishonoured items should be returned to a different financial institution. See the proceeding sections in this chapter for related procedures.
Important
:	You canГўВҖВҷt recall or edit a deposit once itГўВҖВҷs been approved or transmitted.
9.6	Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit
To locate a Branch Capture Deposit:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Select
Deposits
or
Deposit List
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select ГўВҖВңDepositsГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңDeposit ListГўВҖВқ
Note:	If you are assigned the Reviewer and/or Supervisor User type, select the View icon to access a specific locationГўВҖВҷs Deposit List page. Alternatively, select
All Deposits
to access deposits at all assigned locations. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Select the View Icon (If applicable)
The Deposit List page defaults to the current day. To view deposits from a previous day, modify the deposit list filter as follows. See Figure 10.
Select the List of Deposits drop-down menu.
Select the appropriate deposit timeframe or deposit state. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit, for deposit state descriptions.
Figure 10: Select the Deposit Timeframe or Deposit State
Depending on your user type(s), the deposit ownership, the deposit state, and the options available in the Tasks column will vary. See Figure 11. The following Deposit List tasks are available:
ГўВ‘
View Deposit Summary
ГўВҖВ“ View summary information for the deposit.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
View/Edit Deposit Details
ГўВҖВ“ Capture Users can view and/or edit a deposit that they create or were assigned that isnГўВҖВҷt approved by a Reviewer User or transmitted by a Supervisor User. Reviewer and Supervisor Users can view, approve (or disapprove), and/or transmit deposits. See the related sections in this chapter for more information.
ГўВ‘Вў
Delete Deposit
ГўВҖВ“ Capture Users Only. Users can delete a deposit that they create or were assigned if it isnГўВҖВҷt approved by a Reviewer User or transmitted by a Supervisor User. See Section 9.13, Deleting a Branch Capture Deposit, for more information.
Figure 11: Deposit List Tasks
To view deposit details:
Select the Edit icon on the appropriate deposit. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Select the Edit Icon
View the deposit details on the Deposit Item List page. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: View Deposit Item List Page
9.7	Editing Branch Capture Deposit Information
Capture Users can view and edit deposit information for deposits they create or are assigned. You can only edit deposits that arenГўВҖВҷt approved or transmitted. This section describes how to edit deposit information. See Section 9.8, Viewing, Adding, Editing, or Deleting Branch Capture Deposit Items, for procedures on modifying individual deposit items.
To edit deposit information:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select
Edit Deposit
. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Select ГўВҖВңEdit DepositГўВҖВқ
Capture Users can edit the following deposit information:
ГўВ‘
Location
ГўВҖВ“ The depositing branch location (if you have access to more than one location)
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Account
ГўВҖВ“ The deposit type (CAD, USD, CSB)
ГўВ‘Вў
Deposit Reference 1-3
ГўВҖВ“ Information about the deposit
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Declared Amount
ГўВҖВ“ The deposit total
Make the change, then select
Save Changes
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Modifying Deposit Information
9.8	Viewing, Adding, Editing, or Deleting Branch Capture Deposit Items
Capture Users can view or delete deposit items, add items to a deposit, or edit an itemГўВҖВҷs details for deposits that they create or are assigned. You can only edit deposits that arenГўВҖВҷt approved or transmitted. Reviewer and Supervisor Users can view deposits items for deposits created by all users at assigned locations.
Important
:	Once a deposit has been approved or transmitted, it canГўВҖВҷt be edited or recalled.
Viewing Deposit Items
To view deposit items:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select the Edit icon to view a deposit item. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Select  the Edit Icon
View the item image and details. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Viewing Item Image and Details
Use the following options on image toolbar options to better view the image. See Figure 18.
ГўВ‘
Zoom in
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Zoom out
ГўВ‘Вў
Zoom to signature
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Zoom to endorsement
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Zoom to bank of first deposit
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Rotate image right
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Rotate the image left
ГўВ‘В§
View front
ГўВ‘ВЁ
View back
ГўВ‘В©
Reverse view to toggle between the regular and negative image view
Figure 18: Image Toolbar
Adding Items to a Deposit
Capture Users can add items to a current deposit or reopen a deposit to add items if the deposit is in an Open-Balanced or Open-Approved state. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit, for deposit state descriptions.
Add items to a deposit as follows:
Select
Reopen Deposit
if applicable. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Reopen Deposit
Select
Add Items
. See Figure 20. Place the additional items in the scanner hopper. The additional items display on the Deposit Item List page.
Figure 20: Select ГўВҖВңAdd ItemsГўВҖВқ
Editing Deposit Items
Capture users edit a deposit to correct amount misreads or enter additional information about an item (e.g., route and transit for items that should be returned to another financial institution if dishonoured). See Section 9.11, Entering Return Information in Branch Capture, for more information.
Edit an item as follows:
Select the Edit icon on the appropriate item as previously described.
Modify the appropriate field(s). The following fields are editable. See Figure 21.
Note: 	Additional fields are editable if items contain deposit errors. See Section 9.9, Correcting Branch Capture Deposit Errors, for more information.
ГўВ‘
Amount
ГўВҖВ“ The deposit itemГўВҖВҷs amount. Edit this field to correct an amount error. See Section 9.9, Correcting Branch Capture Deposit Errors, for more information.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Depositor Transit
(5-digit) ГўВҖВ“ Complete if items should be returned to a different financial institution if dishonoured. Enter the appropriate return transit for the return of an inter-institutional deposit item, ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs deposit item, Receiver General Concentrator Account deposit item, or Provincial Concentrator Account deposit item. Used in conjunction with the Depositor Route. See Section 9.11, Entering Return information in Branch Capture, for more information. Leave blank if dishonoured items should be returned your financial institution.
ГўВ‘Вў
Depositor Route
(3-digit) ГўВҖВ“ Complete if items should be returned to a different financial institution if dishonoured. Enter the appropriate depositor route for the return of an inter-institutional deposit item, ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs deposit item, Receiver General Concentrator Account deposit item, or a Provincial Concentrator Account deposit item. Used in conjunction with the Depositor Transit. See Section 9.11, Entering Return information in Branch Capture, for more information. Leave blank if dishonoured items should be returned your financial institution.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Depositor PAN/Account Number
(30 character) ГўВҖВ“ Optional. Enter the depositor PAN or account number for ATM deposit items. This information isnГўВҖВҷt transmitted with the deposit, but it appears on the deposit item detail report. See Section 13.2, Generating a Branch Capture Deposit Item Details Report, for more information.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Item Reference
(30 character) ГўВҖВ“ Optional. Enter additional information about the deposit item. This information isnГўВҖВҷt transmitted with the deposit, but it appears on the deposit item detail report. See Section 13.2, Generating a Branch Capture Deposit Item Details Report, for more information.
Figure 21: Edit Item Page
Deleting Items from a Deposit
Capture Users can delete an item from the Edit Item page or from the Deposit Item List page.
Note: 	If you need to delete an entire deposit, see Section 9.13, Deleting a Branch Capture Deposit.
Delete an item from a deposit from the Edit Item page as follows:
Select
Delete
, then select
OK
to confirm the deletion. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Deleting a Deposit Item from the Edit Item Page
Delete an item from the Deposit Item List page as follows:
Select the Delete icon on the appropriate item, then select
OK
to confirm the deletion. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Deleting a Deposit item from the Deposit Item List Page
Note:	You must update the depositГўВҖВҷs Declared Amount field after removing items from a deposit. See Section 9.10, Balancing a Branch Capture Deposit, for procedures.
Select
Return to Deposit List
to return to your list of deposits. See Figure 24.
9.9	Correcting Branch Capture Deposit Errors
Branch Capture assesses the MICR, amount, and the image quality of each deposit item, and compares them against other items in the database to detect potential duplicates. When Branch Capture detects an error, the item is highlighted in red and an alert displays. See Figure 22. Capture Users correct deposit errors for deposits that they create or are assigned.
Deposit items can have the following error types:
Potential duplicate
Failed image quality
Piggyback items
Missing, incomplete, or incorrect amount
Unknown item
Figure 24: Deposit Item Error
Viewing and Navigating Deposit Errors
The following are general procedures for viewing and navigating deposit errors. See the proceeding subsections for procedures on correcting specific error types.
View a deposit error as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select the alert symbol in the Errors column. See Figure 25.
Figure 25: Select Alert Symbol
View the error message. See Figure 26. Deposit items can have more than one error type (e.g., amount error, unknown item error). Additional error type(s) display after you resolve the current error.
Figure 26: Navigating the Edit Item Page
Select
Next Error
after youГўВҖВҷve resolved the current error or use the arrows to navigate to the next item. See Figure 24. Navigating to the next item automatically saves changes made to the current item. You canГўВҖВҷt navigate to the next item without correcting all outstanding item errors.
Note:	Capture users must resolve all error messages before proceeding with the deposit.
The following subsections describe how to resolve specific error types.
Resolving a Potential Duplicate Error
Potential duplicate errors occur when the Branch Capture application detects a match to another item in the current deposit, or in a previously processed deposit.
Note:	The Branch Capture application only detects potential duplicates on items processed by your financial institution and other Central 1 clients within 180 days. Branch Capture canГўВҖВҷt detect items processed by other financial institutions (e.g., RBC, CIBC). Potential duplicates are detected on a best-efforts basis and arenГўВҖВҷt guaranteed.
Resolve a potential duplicate error as follows:
Select the appropriate item with an error as previously described.
Select
Duplicate
. See Figure 27.
Figure 27: Select ГўВҖВңDuplicateГўВҖВқ
Compare the items in the Duplicate Item Details section if available.
Important
:	Capture Users can view the duplicate itemГўВҖВҷs image and deposit details if the original item was scanned at location(s) to which theyГўВҖВҷve been granted access. See Figure 28 for an example. If the item was deposited via mobile deposit, at a location to which a Capture User isnГўВҖВҷt granted access, or by a different Central 1 client, the second itemГўВҖВҷs image and deposit details are blank. See Figure 29 for an example.
Figure 28: Duplicate Item at a Location to Which the User Is Granted Access
Figure 29: Duplicate Item at a Location to Which the User IsnГўВҖВҷt Granted Access
Select
Return to Edit Item
to go back to the Edit Item page. See Figure 30.
Figure 30: Select ГўВҖВңReturn to Edit ItemГўВҖВқ
Caution
:	Carefully review all duplicate items. If the deposit has more than one duplicate item, ensure that the items in the deposit werenГўВҖВҷt previously processed in another deposit. If items were previously processed, delete the duplicate items from the deposit, or delete the entire deposit as appropriate. See Section 9.13, Deleting a Branch Capture Deposit, for procedures. Follow your internal policy for managing potential duplicates if the matching item information isnГўВҖВҷt available to you.
Delete a suspected duplicate item as follows:
Select
Delete
, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 31.
Figure 31: Delete Duplicate Item
Note:	You must update the depositГўВҖВҷs Declared Amount field after removing items from a deposit. See Section 9.10, Balancing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Override the potential duplicate warning and accept the item for processing as follows:
Important
:	Follow your internal policy on overriding potential duplicate item warnings.
Select the Override Duplicates check box, then select
Save,
or select
Next Error
to correct the next deposit error, if applicable. See Figure 32.
Figure 32: Override Duplicate Error
Resolving a Failed Image Quality Error
You must capture and transmit good quality images for processing. Financial institutions can dishonour items with poor image quality up to 90 days after clearing. If an image is reversed, upside down, bent, folded, piggyback, askew, or an otherwise poor image, the item must be deleted from the deposit and re-scanned. Items that fail Branch Capture image quality tests display a ГўВҖВңFailed Image QualityГўВҖВқ error.
Resolve a failed image quality error as follows:
Select the item with an error as previously described.
Note:	 If the itemГўВҖВҷs MICR information wasnГўВҖВҷt captured, delete and rescan the item.
Review the item. If it appears acceptable, select
Failed Image Quality
to help determine why the item failed image quality tests. See Figure 33.
Figure 33: Select ГўВҖВңFailed Image QualityГўВҖВқ
Select
All
to view the results for all tests. Use the Back/Front option to toggle between the back and front of the image. See Figure 34. Select
Return to Edit Item
to resolve the error.
Figure 34: Viewing the Image Quality Test Results
Decide if you should delete and rescan the item or if you should override the failed image quality error and submit the item for processing.
Delete a failed image quality item from the deposit as follows:
Select
Delete
, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 35.
Figure 35: Deleting a Failed Image Quality Item
Obtain the original item from the scanner output tray, then rescan the item. See Section 9.8, Viewing, Adding, Editing, or Deleting Branch Capture Deposit Items, for procedures.
Override the image quality warning and accept the item for processing as follows:
Important
:	Follow your internal policy for managing failed image quality items.
Select the Override Image Quality check box, then select
Save,
or select
Next Error
to correct the next deposit error, if applicable. See Figure 36.
.
Figure 36: Overriding an Image Quality Error
Resolving Piggyback Items
If two or more items are imaged together through the scanner, itГўВҖВҷs called a ГўВҖВңpiggybackГўВҖВқ. See Figure 37. Piggybacks contain the front image of one item with either the front or back of another item behind it. Depending on how closely aligned items are when imaged, piggybacks may be obvious or subtle. When reviewing images, scrutinize them and ensure that there is only one item captured per image. While the Branch Capture application attempts to detect piggybacks, they may not display a Failed Image Quality or Unknown Items error. See Section 7.13, Reducing Piggybacks and Scanners Jams, for information on minimizing piggybacks.
Note:	When thereГўВҖВҷs a piggyback in a deposit, the deposit is out of balance as both item amounts arenГўВҖВҷt read.
Figure 37: Piggyback Item
Important
:	 Piggybacks arenГўВҖВҷt eligible for clearing. DonГўВҖВҷt include them in a deposit. The front and back of each item in a deposit must be captured.
Resolve Piggyback items as follows:
Review the images in the deposit to identify the piggyback.
Locate the physical items included in the piggyback.
Delete the piggyback item from the deposit and rescan all items included in the piggyback. See Section 9.8, Viewing, Editing and Deleting Branch Capture Deposit Items for procedures.
Important
: 	DonГўВҖВҷt just rescan the item(s) behind the front item, you must ensure the front and back of each item is captured.
Resolving Amount Errors or Misread Amounts
Branch Capture uses handwriting recognition to determine the item amount. Amount errors occur when the application canГўВҖВҷt read the amount or canГўВҖВҷt determine if the amount read is correct. See Figure 38 for an example of an amount error. Sometimes the application will misread an amount. In that event an error wonГўВҖВҷt display, but the deposit will be out of balance.
If the application canГўВҖВҷt read the amount, the Amount field is highlighted in red and is blank.
If the application wants the user to confirm the amount captured is correct, the field is highlighted in red and contains an amount the user must verify.
If the application misreads the amount, an error wonГўВҖВҷt display; however, the deposit will be out of balance. You must determine which item was misread and correct the item amount.
Figure 38: Amount Error
Resolve an amount error as follows:
Select the item with an error as previously described.
Review the item image to ensure the amount error wasnГўВҖВҷt due to a poor-quality image. If so, delete the item from the deposit, then rescan it. See Section 9.8, Viewing, Adding, Editing, or Deleting Branch Capture Items, for procedures.
If the image is acceptable, enter or confirm the value in the Amount field as appropriate, then select
Save,
or select
Next Error
to correct the next deposit error if applicable. See Figure 39. Use the item image or the original item to obtain the correct amount.
Resolve a misread amount as follows:
Locate the misread item by comparing the physical items to the amounts displaying in Branch Capture, or by comparing captured images to the amount on the Edit Item page.
Review the item image to ensure the misread wasnГўВҖВҷt due to a poor-quality image. If so, delete the item from the deposit, then rescan it. See Section 9.8, Viewing, Adding, Editing, or Deleting Branch Capture Items, for procedures. If the image is acceptable, update the appropriate itemГўВҖВҷs amount as previously described.
Important
:	You must correct amount misreads to balance the deposit. DonГўВҖВҷt update the declared amount to force-balance a deposit. An out-of-balance deposit will result in a deposit adjustment. See Section 17.3, Deposit Adjustments, for more information.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Figure 39: Update or Confirm Item Amount
Resolving an Unknown Item Error
As items are scanned, Branch Capture reads the MICR line and assigns an item type to each deposit item. If the item type canГўВҖВҷt be determined due to a poor MICR read, failed image quality, or a non-standard MICR format (e.g., travelers cheques, or money orders), the item displays an ГўВҖВңUnknown ItemГўВҖВқ error.
Resolve an unknown item error as follows:
Review the item carefully to determine why the application couldnГўВҖВҷt assign an item type. Assess the image quality to ensure the error wasnГўВҖВҷt due to a poor-quality image. If so, delete the item from the deposit, then rescan it.
Delete an unknown item from the deposit as follows:
Select
Delete
, then select
OK
when prompted. See Figure 40.
Obtain the original item and rescan it. See Section 9.8, Viewing, Adding, Editing, or Deleting Branch Capture Deposit Items, for procedures.
Figure 40: Deleting an Unknown Item
If the item continues to display an unknown item error, but the image quality is good, you can assign an item type and submit the item for processing.
Assign the appropriate item type as follows:
Select the Unknown drop-down menu, then select the appropriate item type. Select
Save
or select
Next Error
to correct the next deposit error if applicable. See Figure 41. Use the descriptions and item type samples that follow to determine the correct item type.
Important
:	If you select an incorrect item type, donГўВҖВҷt save the change to the item. Select
Cancel
, then select the applicable item type. The item type canГўВҖВҷt be edited once youГўВҖВҷve saved it. If that occurs, delete and rescan the item as previously described.
Figure 41: Select Item Type Classification
Canadian and US Dollar Sample Items
The following are descriptions and samples of common Canadian and US dollar MICR lines. Use this information to determine the correct item type to assign to an unknown item.
Canadian Dollar Items
Canadian dollar cheques drawn on a financial institution domiciled in Canada
(Personal or Business size)
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Canadian Check
. This includes MICR-encoded Canada Savings and Canada Premium Bonds, Government Warrants, and Canadian funds travelers cheques. Typical MICR format: serial number, transit number, account number. See Figures 42 and Figure 43 for samples.
Note:	CAD cheques may or may not contain a serial number.
Figure 42: Canadian Dollar Personal Cheque Drawn on a Financial Institution Domiciled in Canada
Figure 43: Canadian Dollar Business Cheque Drawn on a Financial Institution Domiciled in Canada
US Dollar Items
The three most common US Dollar item types are as follows.
Note:	US item types are differentiated by MICR variables and format.
US dollar cheques drawn on a financial institution
domiciled in Canada
(Personal or Business size)
ГўВҲВ’
Select
CDN Cheque US Funds
. Typical MICR format: serial number, transit number, account number, tran code (usually ГўВҖВң45ГўВҖВқ). See Figure 44 and Figure 45 for samples.
Figure 44: US Dollar Personal Cheque Drawn on a Financial Institution Domiciled in Canada
Figure 45: US Dollar Business Cheque Drawn on a Financial Institution Domiciled in Canada
Important:
Most US dollar items drawn on a Canadian financial institution have a transaction code (TC) of ГўВҖВң45ГўВҖВқ in the MICR line, identifying the item as being in USD. However, not all USD items will have this TC, and may be incorrectly identified as Canadian dollar items. Examples are:
ГўВҖВ“ Canadian dollar item drawn on a Canadian financial institution that has ГўВҖВңUS FundsГўВҖВқ or similar wording hand-written on the cheque to designate the items as US dollar, and
ГўВҖВ“ US dollar item drawn on a Canadian financial institution that is missing the TC in the MICR.
In both instances, the Branch Capture application will identify the item type as a ГўВҖВңCanadian CheckГўВҖВқ. Review the image, ensure that the item is in US funds, and include it in a US dollar deposit. You arenГўВҖВҷt required to reclassify the item. The item will process in US dollars.
US dollar personal cheque drawn on a US financial institution
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Personal Check
. Personal size. This includes US Funds TravelerГўВҖВҷs cheques, which generally donГўВҖВҷt contain a serial number and donГўВҖВҷt follow the typical US Personal cheque MICR format. Two typical MICR formats: transit number, serial number, account number or (transit number, account number, serial number). See Figure 46 and Figure 47.
Figure 46: US Funds Personal Cheque ГўВҖВ“ Serial Number After Account Number
Figure 47: US Funds Personal Cheque ГўВҖВ“ Serial Number Between Transit and Account Number
US dollar business cheques drawn on a US financial institution
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Business Check
. Business size. Typical MICR format: serial number, transit number, account number. See Figure 48.
Figure 48: US Funds Business Cheque ГўВҖВ“ Serial Number Between Transit and Account Number
There are four other USD item types in the drop-down menu. These items arenГўВҖВҷt expected to be common. There are no available samples.
US Personal Check EPC
ГўВҖВ“ Personal size. Contains a single digit EPC to the left of the transit number. Two typical MICR formats: EPC, transit number, serial number, account number or EPC, transit number, account number, serial number.
US Business Check EPC
ГўВҖВ“ Drawn on a US financial institution, issues by the Navy Fed. Typical MICR format: 256074974, serial number, account number or 256074974, account number, serial number.
US Multi-Dash Account
ГўВҖВ“ Drawn on a US financial institution. Contains multiple dashes. Not typically used.
US Personal Check Navy Fed
ГўВҖВ“ Drawn on a US financial institution, issues by the Navy Fed. Typical MICR format: 256074974, serial number, account number or 256074974, account number, serial number.
9.10	Balancing a Branch Capture Deposit
Capture Users must ensure deposits are balanced and error-free before completing the deposit.
A balanced deposits displays a value of ГўВҖВң0.00ГўВҖВқ in green text in the Balancing difference field and the ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ button is green. See FigureВ 49.
Figure 49: Balanced Deposit
An out-of-balance deposit displays the out-of-balance value in red text in the Balancing difference field and the ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ button is red. See Figure 50.
Figure 50: Out-of-Balance Deposit
Deposits can be out of balance due to errors in your tape-listing, entering an incorrect declared amount, an item amount misread, or entering the incorrect amount when resolving an amount error. You canГўВҖВҷt complete an out-of-balance deposit. Capture users must determine why a deposit is out of balance and correct the error(s).
Balance a deposit as follows:
Ensure that you correct all deposit errors. See Section 9.9, Correcting Branch Capture Deposit Errors.
Compare the items in the Scanned Item section to your tape-listing, the scanned images, or to the physical items. If an item amount was misread or entered incorrectly, rescan the item or modify the item amount. See Section 9.9, Correcting Branch Capture Deposit Errors, for procedures.
If you entered the total value of deposit items incorrectly when you created the deposit or made an error when listing the items, modify the value in ГўВҖВңDeclared amountГўВҖВқ field. See Section 9.9, Viewing, Editing, and Deleting Branch Capture Deposit Items, for procedures.
Important
: 	DonГўВҖВҷt modify the value in the ГўВҖВңDeclared amountГўВҖВқ field to match the value in the ГўВҖВңCurrent amountГўВҖВқ field without locating the item causing the deposit to be out of balance. ThatГўВҖВҷs force-balancing a deposit. Force balanced deposits will result in a deposit adjustment.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
9.11	Entering Return Information in Branch Capture
Capture Users enter return information in Branch Capture for items that must be returned to a different financial institution if dishonoured. The information entered populates the return fields on Clearing Replacement Documents (CRDs or image exchange files that are exchanged with other financial institutions). Ensure the deposit is error-free and balanced before entering return information.
Important:
If you donГўВҖВҷt enter return information in Branch Capture, your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs transit information populates the itemГўВҖВҷs return fields. If the item is dishonoured, itГўВҖВҷs returned to your financial institution.
This section provides instructions for entering return information for the following deposit items. This may not be an inclusive list. Follow your internal procedures where appropriate.
ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs Deposits
(BC only) ГўВҖВ“ Cheques included in ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs deposits. See the ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs Deposits Part (BC) for more information on preparing ICBC agentГўВҖВҷs deposits.
Inter-Institutional Deposit Items
ГўВҖВ“ Cheques deposited at your financial institution by a customer from a different financial institution (e.g., ATM, ICU deposits).
Receiver General Concentrator Account Deposit Items
ГўВҖВ“ Cheques included in Receiver General Concentrator holding account deposits (e.g., deposits from Federal departments or agencies). See Branch Capture ГўВҖВ“ Processing Receiver General Deposits for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Concentrator Account, Form 5976, for more information on preparing Receiver General Account deposit items.
Provincial Concentrator Account Deposit Items
ГўВҖВ“ Cheques included in Provincial Concentrator holding account deposits (e.g., deposits from the Province of BC, a Province of BC Services office, or a Province of BC regulated line of business). See the Provincial Concentrator Account Deposits Part for more information on preparing Provincial Concentrator Account deposit items.
Enter return information as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select the Edit icon on the first deposit item. See Figure 51.
Figure 51: Select the Edit Icon
Complete the Depositor Transit and Depositor Route fields for the appropriate deposit type as follows. See Figure 52.
ICBC AgentГўВҖВҷs deposit items (BC only)
ГўВҲВ’
Enter ГўВҖВң09150ГўВҖВқ in the Depositor Transit field and ГўВҖВң003ГўВҖВқ in the Depositor Route field.
Inter-institution (non-member ATM or ICU) deposit items
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the issuing financial institutionГўВҖВҷs 5-digit branch number in the Depositor Transit field and 3-digit institution number in the Depositor Route field. Use the return stamp information on the back of the original item or use the image tool bar in Branch Capture to view the back of the image.
Receiver General Concentrator Account deposit items
ГўВҲВ’
Enter ГўВҖВң71620ГўВҖВқ in the Depositor Transit field and ГўВҖВң809ГўВҖВқ in the Depositor Route field.
Provincial Concentrator Account deposit items (BC only)
ГўВҲВ’
Enter
ГўВҖВң
00090
ГўВҖВқ
in the Depositor Transit field and
ГўВҖВң
010
ГўВҖВқ
in the Depositor Route field.
Figure 52: Complete the Depositor Transit and Depositor Route Fields
Use the arrows to move to the next deposit item. Moving to the next item automatically saves changes made to the current item. DonГўВҖВҷt navigate to the next item without entering the return information for the current item. Select
Save
when youГўВҖВҷve finished entering the return information for all items in the deposit. See Figure 53.
Figure 53: Navigate to Next Item
Related Information
Branch Capture ГўВҖВ“ Processing Receiver General Deposits for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Concentrator Account,
Form 5976
9.12	Adding and Viewing Branch Capture Deposit Notes
Capture Users, Reviewer Users, and Supervisor Users can add deposit notes to communicate information about the deposit to another user or to record additional information about a deposit. For example, Reviewer Users can use notes for disapproved deposits to describe deposit errors, provide correction instructions, or provide feedback on a deposit. You can only add deposit notes to deposits in an ГўВҖВңopenГўВҖВқ state (e.g., Open-balanced, Open-disapproved) and if the deposit is editable by the user. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit, for deposit state descriptions.
Note:	You canГўВҖВҷt edit or delete a deposit note once added. Once a deposit is approved or transmitted, you can only read deposit notes.
Add a deposit note as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select
Notes
. See Figure 54.
Figure 54: Select ГўВҖВңNotesГўВҖВқ
Enter the deposit note text, then select
Add Note
. See Figure 55. An information symbol displays to indicate the deposit contains notes. See Figure 56.

Figure 56: Deposit with Notes
Viewing a Deposit Note
View a deposit note as follows:
Select
Notes
. See Figure 57.
Figure 57: Select ГўВҖВңNotesГўВҖВқ
View the note text. See Figure 58. You can respond by adding a new note as previously described.
Figure 58: Viewing a Deposit Note

Capture users can delete a deposit that they create or are assigned from the Deposit Item List page, or the Deposit List page.
Note:	Deposits in an Open-Approved state canГўВҖВҷt be deleted. Supervisors must transmit Open-Approved deposits to Central 1.
Delete a deposit from the Deposit Item List page as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select
Delete Deposit
, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 59.

Delete a deposit from the Deposit List as follows:

Select the Delete icon, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 60.

9.14	Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit
ItГўВҖВҷs important to carefully review deposits before submitting them to Central 1 for processing. Capture Users must review deposits that they create or are assigned to correct errors, verify the deposit items are appropriate for the deposit currency, and ensure the deposit is balanced. Reviewer Users and Supervisor Users must carefully review deposits before approving, disapproving, and/or transmitting them.
The Deposit Item List page contains two sections: the Deposit Information section and the Scanned Items section. See Figure 62.
Deposit Information Section
The Deposit Information section displays the following deposit summary information:
ГўВ‘
Deposit State
ГўВҖВ“ The depositГўВҖВҷs current processing state. The options are as follows:
ГўВҲВ’
Open-Processing
: The initial deposit state as the Capture User scans deposit items.
ГўВҲВ’
Open-Balanced
: Indicates the deposit is balanced, no error tasks are outstanding, and a Capture User selected ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ. The deposit remains in this state until itГўВҖВҷs approved or disapproved by a Reviewer User. This state doesnГўВҖВҷt apply to deposits created by Capture Users who are also assigned the Reviewer and/or Supervisor user type.
ГўВҲВ’
Open-Jammed
: An exception state indicating a misfeed. See Section 7.14, Resolving Scanner Jams.
ГўВҲВ’
Open-Incomplete
: Indicates a deposit is in progress. This state is set when a Capture User re-opens a balanced or disapproved deposit to add additional items or correct errors. This state can also be set if a Capture User selects ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ on a deposit that contains errors or is out of balance. The deposit remains in this state until a Capture User balances the deposit, corrects errors, and selects ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ.
ГўВҲВ’
Open-Approved
: Indicates a Reviewer User approved a Capture UserГўВҖВҷs deposit. Approved deposits canГўВҖВҷt be modified or deleted and must be transmitted to Central 1 for processing by a Supervisor User. See Chapter 10, Disapproving or Approving a Branch Capture Deposit, for more information. This state doesnГўВҖВҷt apply to deposits created by Capture Users who are also assigned the Reviewer and Supervisor user types.
ГўВҲВ’
Open-Disapproved
: Indicates a Reviewer User disapproved a Capture UserГўВҖВҷs deposit. Disapproved deposits must be re-opened for further processing by the Capture User who created or is assigned the deposit. See Chapter 10, Disapproving or Approving a Branch Capture Deposit, for more information. This state doesnГўВҖВҷt apply to deposits created by Capture Users who are also assigned the Reviewer and/or Supervisor user type.
ГўВҲВ’
Received
: Indicates the deposit was successfully transmitted by a Supervisor User and is ready for processing at Central 1. See Chapter 11, Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit, for more information.
ГўВҲВ’
Receive Failed
: An exception state indicating the deposit was not successfully transmitted for processing. DonГўВҖВҷt rescan the items in the deposit. Contact your provincial central for assistance.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Declared Amount
ГўВҖВ“ The deposit total that the Capture User enters when creating the deposit.
ГўВ‘Вў
Current Amount
ГўВҖВ“ The deposit items total as recognised by the Branch Capture Application. As cheques scan, the application reads the item amounts and provides a total.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Balancing Difference
ГўВҖВ“ The difference between the declared and current amounts.
Scanned Items Section
The Scanned Items section displays the following details:
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Scanned Items
ГўВҖВ“
The number of items in the deposit, including the virtual deposit slip.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Errors
ГўВҖВ“ If Branch Capture detects an error, an alert symbol displays beside the item and itГўВҖВҷs highlighted in red. The Capture User must review the item and correct the error(s).
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Tasks
ГўВҖВ“ The tasks the user can perform on an item. The available actions vary based on the usersГўВҖВҷ assigned user type(s) and the depositГўВҖВҷs state (e.g., if the deposit is open for editing or completed). Tasks include:
ГўВҲВ’
Edit Item (pencil icon). Available to all users that can view or action a deposit.
ГўВҲВ’
View Item History (blue checkmark icon) . Available to Reviewer and Supervisor Users.
ГўВҲВ’
Delete Item (red X icon) . Available to Capture Users.
ГўВ‘В§
Item Type
ГўВҖВ“ The item type that the application assigns when reading the itemГўВҖВҷs MICR line (e.g., Canadian Check, Canadian Cheque in US Funds, US check). The first item type in the deposit is the virtual deposit slip that Branch Capture generates.
Note:	The virtual deposit slip provides deposit details (e.g., account information, deposit date, number of items in the deposit, deposit total, deposit reference information, if applicable).
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Post Amount
ГўВҖВ“ The item amount as read by the application.
ГўВ‘В©
Capture
Sequence
ГўВҖВ“ The sequence number the application assigns to individual items as they appear in the deposit.
ГўВ‘ВӘ
Account
ГўВҖВ“ The account number captured from the itemГўВҖВҷs MICR line.
ГўВ‘В«
Routing Transit
ГўВҖВ“ The transit number captured from the itemГўВҖВҷs MICR line.
Figure 62: Deposit Item List
Users should particularly focus on the Errors and Item Type columns on the Deposit Item List page to ensure the deposit is error-free and the deposit contains the appropriate item types for the deposit (e.g., Canadian items in a Canadian dollar deposit). You must also confirm the deposit is balanced. See the related sections in this chapter for procedures on correcting errors and balancing a deposit.
Reviewing the Item Type Column
Review item types as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Review the Item Type column to ensure the recognized or assigned item type for each item is appropriate for the deposit. For example, if the deposit was created in Canadian dollars, the item type for each item should be ГўВҖВңCanadian CheckГўВҖВқ.
ГўВҲВ’
If the deposit contains an item in the wrong currency (e.g., a US cheque in a Canadian deposit), delete the item, adjust the balance, and put the physical item in the correct deposit type. See Section 9.8, Viewing, Adding, Editing, or Deleting Branch Capture Deposit Items, and Section 9.10, Balancing a Branch Capture Deposit. Depositing items in the incorrect currency will result in a deposit adjustment.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Important
: 	Most US dollar items drawn on a Canadian financial institution have a ГўВҖВң45ГўВҖВқ transaction code (TC) in the MICR line, identifying the item as being in USD. Not all USD items will have this TC, however, and may be identified as Canadian dollar items in the Item Type column.
Since Branch Capture assesses a chequesГўВҖВҷ MICR to assign an item type (CAD or USD), there are two instances (not including misreads) when items are assigned the Canadian dollar item type but should be included in a US dollar deposit:
ГўВҲВ’
Canadian dollar items drawn on a Canadian financial institution with ГўВҖВңUS FundsГўВҖВқ or similar wording hand-written on the cheque to designate the items as US dollar, and
ГўВҲВ’
US dollar items drawn on a Canadian financial institution missing the TC ГўВҖВң45ГўВҖВқ in the MICR.
In both instances, the Branch Capture application assigns the item type as a ГўВҖВңCanadian CheckГўВҖВқ. Review the image, ensure that the item is in US funds, and include it in the US dollar deposit. YouГўВҖВҷre not required to reclassify the item.
Important
: 	US dollar items canГўВҖВҷt be included in a Canadian deposit if ГўВҖВңCanadian fundsГўВҖВқ has been written on the cheque. See Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, for more information.
Reviewing Item History
Reviewer and Supervisor Users can view changes that a Capture User made to a deposit item as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select the Item History icon. See Figure 63.
Figure 63: Select ГўВҖВңView Item HistoryГўВҖВқ Icon
Review the changes made in the User Action field (e.g., accepting a suspected duplicate item, overriding image quality errors, reclassifying unknown item errors). Recognition/Image Quality results and an item image also display. See Figure 64.
Figure 64: View Item History Information
Ensure the deposit is error-free and balanced. If the deposit is error-free and balanced, the balancing difference on the Deposit Item List page is ГўВҖВң0.00ГўВҖВқ in green text. Additionally, for Capture Users, the ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ button is green. See Figure 65. See Section 9.15, Completing a Branch Capture Deposit, for information on completing deposits.
Figure 65: Balanced and Error-free Deposit
9.15	Completing a Branch Capture Deposit
Completing a Branch Capture deposit indicates that the Capture User declares that the deposit is error-free, balanced, and ready for review, approval, and/or transmission to Central 1 for processing.
Branch Capture allows user types and their associated tasks to be segregated or combined. Segregating or combining the user types changes the workflow and user experience when completing a deposit as follows. See Section 8.2, Branch Capture Workflows and User Types, for more information.
Capture User
ГўВҖВ“ Selecting ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ places the deposit in an Open-Balanced state for a Reviewer User to approve. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit, for deposit state descriptions.
Note:	Users with the Reviewer and/or Supervisor user type(s) must review and/or transmit a Capture UserГўВҖВҷs Open-Balanced deposits.
Capture, Reviewer, and Supervisor user types combined
ГўВҖВ“ Selecting ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ automatically approves and transmits the deposit to Central 1 for processing. Ensure the deposit is error-free and balanced before completing the deposit. Once completed, the deposit canГўВҖВҷt be edited, deleted, or recalled.
Capture and Reviewer user types combined
(this combination isnГўВҖВҷt recommended) ГўВҖВ“ Selecting ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ automatically places the deposit in an Open-Approved state. Approved deposits canГўВҖВҷt be edited or deleted. The Supervisor must transmit Open-Approved deposits to Central 1. The deposit must be error-free and balanced before completing the deposit.
Complete a deposit as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.

Select

, then select
